zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston於1286年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素時，他的父親Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas43歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Stewart36歲。他和Joan Lovel至少有1個兒子。他去世於1330年8月25日在西班牙安達盧希亞馬拉加泰巴享年44歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭素斯 藍納可爾道格拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Black
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blagg
meanings: English: variant of   Black   with voicing of the final consonant.

variants.name: Blake
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which has two quite distinct etymologies. It is both from Old English blæc ‘black’ and from Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ it was thus originally a nickname given to someone with hair or skin that was either remarkably dark or remarkably light. It is now quite popular as a boy's name.

variants.name: Blackie
meanings: Scottish: diminutive of   Black  .
English: nickname for a person with dark eyes, from Middle English blak ‘black’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English blæc + ēage).

variants.name: Blackson
meanings: English: patronymic from the nickname or surname Black + -son. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Block
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a nickname for someone resembling a badger (Middle English broc(k), Old English brocc, of Celtic origin).

variants.name: Blacklock
meanings: English (northern): nickname for someone with dark hair, from Middle English blāk + lok(e) (Old English blæc + locc), although blāk might mean either ‘dark’ or ‘fair’ (see Black ). The meaning ‘dark hair’ is the most probable since this name contrasts with Whitlock .

variants.name: Blatch
meanings: English (Essex, but historically also in south and southwest England): nickname from Middle English blache, bleche ‘pale, pallid, of fair complexion’ (Old English blǣce).

variants.name: Blackett
meanings: English:
nickname for a person with dark or fair hair, from Middle English blak ‘black’ (Old English blæc) or ‘fair’ (Old English blāc) + hed, heved ‘head’. See also Black .
nickname from Middle English blak ‘black’ + hod ‘hood’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston was born in 1286, in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas, was 43 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Stewart, was 36. He had at least 1 son with Joan Lovel. He died on 25 August 1330, in Teba, Málaga, Andalusia, Spain, at the age of 44, and was buried in Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston est né en 1286, à Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Stewart, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Joan Lovel. Il est décédé le 25 août 1330, à Teba, Málaga, Andalucía, Espagne, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston 1286 in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas 43 und seine Mutter Lady Elizabeth Stewart 36. Er hatte mit Joan Lovel mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 25. August 1330 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Teba, Málaga, Andalucía, Spanien und wurde in Douglas, South Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston è nato il nel 1286 nel Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Stewart, aveva 36 anni. ja.lifeSummary: Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermistonが1286年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス カストルで生まれた時，父Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglasは43歳，母Lady Elizabeth Stewartは36歳であった。彼はJoan Lovelとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1330年8月25日にスペインアンダルクiアムaラガテバで44歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドソウス ラナルクシュアイアドウグラスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston(이)가 1286년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Stewart(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Joan Lovel(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1330년 8월 25일 스페인 앤다루ci아 a라가 테바에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 더글라스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James The Black Douglas Lord of Hermiston родился в 1286 г. в местности доуглас кастл, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Stewart, 36. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Joan Lovel. Он умер 25 августа 1330 г. в местности теба, мaлага, андалукiа, Испания в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности доуглас, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
